intro: Phalacroloma acutifolium, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its spiny stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Phalacroloma acutifolium include alkaloids, such as phalacrolome and acutifoline, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Phalacroloma acutifolium is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis.
